kings_guild.name: The King's Guild
kings_guild.description: Master artisans of the King's Guild each ply a different trade in a different quarter of the city. Work out who's who.
clues: Either Edmund goes with Fletcher, or Weaver goes with Loom — but not both.
Aldric, Saw, Bridgegate, and 7 coins belong to different artisans.
The artisan serving the Baron and the artisan serving the Bishop are in the same guild.
11 coins goes with exactly two of Edmund, Saw, and Bishop.
Either Weaver goes with Loom or someone in the Joiners' Guild goes with Kingsford (but not both).
Countess goes with none of Aldric, Wulfric, or 13 coins.
Between Bishop and Countess, one goes with Bridgegate and the other with 9 coins.
Between Fletcher and 7 coins, one goes with Anvil and the other with Saw.
Anvil goes with at most two of Aldric, Blacksmith, Highrow, Knight, and 7 coins.
Loom goes with at least one of Aldric or Oldwall.

fishing.name: The Fishing Derby
fishing.description: Anglers at the lake-country derby each landed a different fish from a different pond on a different lure. Work out who caught what, and where.
clues: Either Esther goes with Pike, or Trout goes with Spoon — but not both.
Boone, Swimbait, Birchpool, and 2 lb belong to different catches.
The angler fishing with Corn and the angler fishing with Dough are in the same family.
6 lb goes with exactly two of Esther, Swimbait, and Dough.
Either Trout goes with Spoon or someone in the Sunfish Family goes with Stillwater (but not both).
Maggot goes with none of Boone, Wade, or 8 lb.
Between Dough and Maggot, one goes with Birchpool and the other with 4 lb.
Between Pike and 2 lb, one goes with Popper and the other with Swimbait.
Popper goes with at most two of Boone, Bass, Foxglen, Nightcrawler, and 2 lb.
Spoon goes with at least one of Boone or Willowmere.
